summary:
EXP-221 delivers two targeted insertions into WIN-067 detail_evidence addressing both c8 moderate holes. PATCH A (ISS-1385 / c8 H4): elevates a single bold sentence of the Domain 4 r=20,015 km falsification-violation computation from detail_extra paragraph (iii) into the WGS84-tautology paragraph of detail_evidence proper — any reader now sees that Domain 4's own published falsification criterion (>0.1% model error vs WGS84) is self-violated by 4.8× at the dome's only non-degenerate geometrically-motivated evaluation point. PATCH B (ISS-1386 / c8 H5): inserts a new

paragraph between the WGS84-tautology paragraph and the refraction-cascade paragraph explicitly engaging the Mascon ↔ SAA r=0.92 correlation on its own terms — grants the kernel of truth (r=0.92 is a real, high empirical regularity between two global anomaly fields) then shows it does not save the claim because high correlation indicates shared causation (East Antarctic crustal roots + uncompensated sub-lithospheric mantle plume, Pappa et al. 2019 JGR), not a novel dome-specific forecast; the dome supplies no equation mapping disc radius, H(r), or aetheric parameters to either field. Both patches are self-contained substring replacements with unique anchors in the current detail_evidence (dry-run verified 1 occurrence each; post-apply length matches declared). No other WIN-067 fields touched. Carry-forward items (c8 H1 asymmetry, c8 H2 precision slip, c8 H3 Shepherd citation) are NOT addressed by this EXP — they are scoped to separate future expansions per the EXP-221 tracker entry.
